Summary: status effects' duration continues to decrement while on pause screen in single player
Description:
To reproduce, create a world, give yourself a status effect of a set duration, and pause the game for that duration. Once the duration has elapsed, unpause the game and check if the status effect is still applied.

It would be expected that the timer is paused when the game is paused. For example, drinking a potion and pausing the game should not consume the duration of that potion.

Currently, status effects' duration continue to decrement while on the pause screen in single player.

Summary: Hostile mobs spawning during day and not burning | Worlds seem to be dark as if it is inside a cave
Description: Mobs are actively spawning in as if it is night, and the world seems to be very dark as if there is a giant cover of blocks over it. This is occurring inside of any world I open. Additionally, when placing blocks light seems to be created out of nowhere. Sleeping is not possible during day and sleeping during night does not change the state of the bug. Interestingly enough, whilst fooling around on a superflat testing world, no slimes were spawning in. The only hostile mobs spawning in were zombies, skeletons, creepers, spiders, and enderman. No slimes as per usual on a superflat. Also this is happening ONLY to my existing worlds no newly created worlds have this bug, even when leaving the world and rejoining. ALSO it only seems to be happening within certain chunks. Overall this is just an extremely  strange bug which I have not seen anyone talk about. I presume this is an issue with past mods I installed and uninstalled BUT I want to make sure this isn’t an actual bug which I can fix. (I know this probably isn’t important, but I want to be as thorough as possible, so I will say that there are no visible bugs on multiplayer worlds/servers).

Summary: Issue with Arabic Language in Minecraft
Description:
Dear Mojang Support,

I would like to report an issue with the Arabic language support in Minecraft. The current implementation has several problems, including incorrect text alignment, broken word formatting, reversed letters, and an unsuitable font that is hard to read. These issues make the game difficult to enjoy for Arabic-speaking players.

Please consider improving Arabic localization and using a proper Arabic font to ensure a better experience for all users.

Thank you for your attention.

Summary: Possible regression: GuiGraphics#submitOutline doesn't respect matrix transformations
Description:
GuiGraphics#renderOutline has been removed in 25w33a in favor of GuiGraphics#submitOutline.

Any transformations which have been applied to the Matrix3x2fStack (such as translations) are not respected anymore.



Previous example which used to work:

```java
guiGraphics.pose().translate(10, 10);
guiGraphics.renderOutline(0, 0, 5, 5, 0xFFFFFFFF);
```

The above would render a white outline at `10, 10` with width and height each being 5 in white color.



Current state which doesn’t work:

```java
guiGraphics.pose().translate(10, 10);
guiGraphics.submitOutline(0, 0, 5, 5, 0xFFFFFFFF);
```

This outline now doesn’t render at `10, 10` but at `0, 0`.



Expected behavior would probably be for it to render at `10, 10` as was previously the case. The fill command also respects applied transformations, so it would be weird for `submitOutline` not to.

Summary: Carpet disappears when placed above transparent wall piston door upon activation
Description:
When using a transparent wall piston door with Redstone, carpets placed above the moving blocks disappear upon activation.The carpet does not drop as an item, but simply gets deleted.

This issue does not affect every identical build – some piston doors work fine with carpets, others consistently delete them when triggered.



Steps to reproduce

Build a transparent wall piston door (hidden door using sticky pistons and Redstone).

Place carpets on top of the blocks that are part of the piston mechanism.

Activate the Redstone circuit (lever, button, or any power source).

Observe: When the pistons extend or retract, the carpet vanishes.



Expected result

The carpet should remain placed on top of the block even when the piston door is activated.



Actual result

The carpet disappears completely.

It does not drop as an item.

Happens only on certain piston door builds, making it inconsistent.



Additional Notes

Issue only occurs with carpets, not with full blocks.

Seems related to block updates when pistons move blocks beneath carpets.

Tested on Minecraft Java 1.21.8 – may affect other 1.21.x versions as well.
